title: bromus sitchensis var. marginatus
intro: Bromus sitchensis var. marginatus, also known as mountain brome, is a subspecies of the Sitka brome grass, a perennial grass native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve digestive issues. The health benefits of mountain brome include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and digestive disorders. Mountain brome can be used in herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, and is often dried and used in capsule or powder form to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
